data_quality_note: Weekly (13-17 Jul 2026) figures for Nifty 50, Sensex, Nifty IT, Nifty Media, Nifty Pharma and Nifty Healthcare are sourced from market reports and confirmed. Other weekly sector figures are close approximations built from daily sectoral moves reported through the week. Longer-horizon figures (last-2-qtrs, 6-months, 1-year) are reliably anchored for Nifty 50/Sensex using confirmed index closing levels; sector-level longer-horizon figures are directional estimates (marked 'estimated') since verified index-level data for every sector/period was not available via search - treat these as indicative, not exact.

market_overview.fii_activity: FIIs were net sellers for the week (13-17 Jul 2026), with cumulative cash-market net outflow of approx Rs -9,118 crore. Selling was heaviest on 16 Jul (Rs -4,205.56 crore) and eased sharply by Friday (Rs -376.41 crore). FIIs stayed net long in index futures/options into the weekend, however, signaling short-term bullish derivative positioning.
market_overview.dii_activity: DIIs were consistent net buyers through the week, with cumulative net inflow of approx Rs +9,807 crore, largely offsetting FII selling and providing a floor under the market. DIIs rotated out of richly-valued mid- and small-caps into large-cap IT and banking names.
global_triggers: Escalating US-Iran tensions; US airstrikes on Iranian targets and threats to Iran's power grid kept crude oil prices elevated and geopolitical risk premium high
Uncertainty over Strait of Hormuz shipping/transit arrangements following the informal Iran-US truce, with no clear rulebook on passage
Wall Street profit-booking in AI-linked/semiconductor counters dragged US and Asian tech stocks lower mid-week
Firm US dollar and elevated crude prices kept FIIs cautious on emerging markets including India
Rupee under mild pressure (~95.3-96.2/USD) on higher energy import costs
predictions_next_week: Nifty 50 likely to stay range-bound between 24,000 support and 24,500-25,000 resistance zone; a decisive move above 24,500 needed to confirm a stronger up-move
Q1FY27 earnings season to remain the primary domestic driver, with major private banks and Reliance Industries results due, which could set the tone for Financial Services and Energy
Sector rotation likely to continue; Realty, Pharma, Media and Midcap names sit in the RRG 'leading' quadrant and could keep outperforming the broader Nifty 500
FII flows are the key swing factor - a shift back to net buying would be a strong bullish signal, while continued selling on oil/geopolitical worries could cap upside
Watch for continued volatility around any escalation/de-escalation headlines on the Iran-US/Strait of Hormuz situation
market_overview.risk_level: Moderate - India VIX near 13 (relatively low), but oil-price and geopolitical risk (Iran-US, Strait of Hormuz) plus continued FII selling are key overhangs
market_overview.momentum: Improving/neutral-to-positive - Nifty registered its tightest weekly range of 2026 and closed higher, but breadth was narrow and concentrated in large-cap IT/banking names while broader mid/small-caps lagged
market_overview.sentiment: Cautiously optimistic - earnings-led optimism (Tech Mahindra, Jio Financial) offset by weak market breadth, oil-driven geopolitical caution, and continued (though moderating) FII selling
key_stocks: Kotak Mahindra Bank - among top Nifty gainers on earnings optimism, though later fell ~4% on slow sequential loan/deposit growth concerns
Tech Mahindra - rallied on strong Q1 results, led IT sector gains
Jio Financial Services - rallied on strong Q1 results
TCS - among top gainers, supported IT rally
Reliance Industries - gained ~2.4% on promoter stake increase to 50.48% and ahead of Q1 results
Wipro - fell on cautious quarterly guidance
Dr Reddy's Laboratories & Sun Pharma - biggest drags on Nifty Pharma after Novo Nordisk's Wegovy approval in India raised competitive concerns
Cipla - among top Nifty laggards for the week
Grasim Industries (via Aditya Birla Renewables) - announced Rs 17,200 crore ($1.8bn) acquisition of Shell's Sprng Energy (5 GWp renewable capacity), a major corporate action for the week

NIFTY_50.data_quality: confirmed (week, 1yr); estimated (2qtrs, 6mo) from anchored closing levels
drivers: Q1FY27 earnings optimism (Tech Mahindra, Jio Financial)
DII buying offsetting FII selling
Large-cap IT/banking led rally amid weak breadth
positive_news: Nifty posted its tightest weekly trading range of 2026, closing at a one-week high on earnings optimism
negative_news: Broader market underperformed frontline index; Midcap 100 and Smallcap 100 both declined on the week, showing narrow participation
other_notes: Traded in a narrow 368-point weekly band between 24,000 and 24,368

drivers: Reliance Industries, banking and IT heavyweights drove the rally
positive_news: Surged 964.58 points (1.25%) on Friday alone on earnings optimism

drivers: Large-cap led gains diluted slightly by Next 50 underperformance

NIFTY_500.data_quality: estimated - broader index dragged by mid/small-cap weakness this week
drivers: Mid and small-cap underperformance offset large-cap strength
negative_news: Broader market breadth remained weak - more decliners (2,419) than advancers (1,632) on Friday alone

NIFTY_IT.data_quality: confirmed (week); estimated (longer horizons, sector known to be one of the weakest over the past year)

DII rotation into large-cap IT as a value play

negative_news: Wipro issued cautious quarterly guidance, capping some upside
Sector remains well below 52-week highs on a 1-year view amid weak global tech demand
other_notes: Top-performing sector for the week per weekly sector rotation data; moved from 'Lagging' to 'Improving' quadrant on relative rotation graphs
